nicknames: The Heart of Victoria's Thoroughbred Country

main_turf.bias_notes: Counter-Clockwise. Reconstructed and widened to 23 metres, Seymour is a highly regarded, spacious, and fair track. The 380m home straight gives swoopers every possible chance, while the sweeping turns allow for momentum building.
main_turf.post_bias: Generally very fair due to the long runs into the first turn, especially from the 1100m to 1300m sprint chutes which provide a massive straight run before the bend. Wide barriers are far less of a penalty here than at tighter Victorian country tracks.

handicapping_angles.the_fair_track_swoop: Because the track is expansive and the straight is 380m long, one-dimensional front-runners often tire. Heavily upgrade 'Swoopers' (closers) with strong late sectionals.
handicapping_angles.the_chute_advantage: In 1100m, 1200m, and 1300m sprints, do not heavily penalize wide draws. The chute starts provide a very long, straight run before cornering, allowing jockeys plenty of time to slot in and find cover without burning early energy.
handicapping_angles.metro_trainer_targets: Given its proximity to Melbourne and premium surface, elite Metro trainers (Hayes, Freedman, Maher) frequently send high-quality progressive horses here. Respect these runners heavily when dropping from Metro midweek or Saturday grade.
